$comment: ADR-P013 Batches 1-7 + tsp semantics mini-slice + poppy-seeds serving-semantics correction slice + Amendment A1 FNDDS pin batch + Batch F1 (cup) - USDA FoodData Central foodPortion gram-weight provenance. The 'source' block is the immutable SR Legacy pin; 'secondarySources' holds the Amendment A1 FNDDS pin; entries WITHOUT a sourceRef are SR Legacy, entries WITH sourceRef cite their secondary pin (Batch F1: food.polenta; Batch F2: food.pesto, food.tzatziki). Human-reviewed manifest; the fdc-portion-manifest.spec.ts gate enforces schema, provenance, unit compatibility, gram-weight bounds, macro reconciliation against fdcPer100g, and authored-grams === derivedGramsPerServing. Never edit gram values without re-deriving them from the pinned archive.

secondarySources.dataset: USDA FoodData Central — FNDDS Survey Foods (CSV)
secondarySources.verificationNotes: Pinned under ADR-P013 Amendment A1 (pin batch, 2026-07-14) as the newest survey cycle. SHA-256 computed twice from the downloaded archive (reproducible); zip extracts cleanly; contains exactly 5,432 survey_fndds_food records (food.csv), 22,046 food_portion rows, and food_nutrient/measure_unit tables in the same shape the SR gates validate. Pin-only slice: NO matching performed, no manifest entries reference this source yet, no catalog data changed.

tolerances.$comment: Macro-reconciliation gate (ADR-P013 acceptance criterion c). FDC per-100g macros scaled to derivedGramsPerServing must agree with the catalog's authored per-serving macros within these bounds.

entries.reviewNote: Catalog serving is 1 slice; SR portion 'slice' = 32 g. Non-toasted commercial record chosen over toasted/recipe variants. Reconciliation: 80.6 kcal vs 73 authored (delta 7.6 <= 25).
Catalog serving is 1 slice; SR offers regular (32 g), thin (25 g), snack (7 g) — 'slice, regular' matches the authored 81 kcal (reconciles to 82.9 kcal, delta 1.9).
Catalog food is generic 'Sprouted grain bread' (not brand-specific); SR 'Bread, wheat, sprouted' is the closest analytical record. Reconciliation: 71.4 kcal vs 85 authored (delta 13.6 <= 25).

Catalog serving is 2 slices; SR portion is exactly 2 slices = 57 g. 'Unprepared' reconciles (62.7 kcal vs 75, P 11.58 vs 11); the alternate 168383 'cooked, pan-fried' (slice = 13.8 g) FAILS the gate (40.3 kcal vs 75) and was rejected.

Catalog serving is 2 tbsp; SR commercial hummus portion is 1 tbsp = 15 g, so the full serving is 30 g. Commercial record chosen over home-prepared because it reconciles more closely with authored fat/calories. Reconciliation: 71.1 kcal vs 73 authored (within tolerance).

Catalog serving is 1 tbsp; SR grated parmesan portion is 1 tbsp = 5 g. Grated record matches the tablespoon serving better than shredded/low-sodium/topping variants. Reconciliation: 21.0 kcal vs 17 authored (within tolerance).

entries.fdcDescription: Peanut butter, smooth style, without salt

PERRIER is SR Legacy’s carbonated (sparkling) water record — branded, like the kefir pick, noted; the non-carbonated bottled waters are preparation mismatches and club soda carries added sodium salts. density = 29.6 / 29.5735 = 1.0009 g/ml; derived = 240.2 g. Zero-macro policy resolved by owner 2026-07-14.

Direct SR tbsp portion (1 tbsp = 14.9 g). Identified in Batch 2 and held on the zero-macro policy question; sourced now that the owner resolved the policy (2026-07-14). Reconciliation trivially passes (3.1 kcal est vs authored 0).

Batch 7 (owner-authorized density mini-batch). Raw record = exact fresh preparation match. density = 244 g / 236.588 ml (cup row) = 1.0313 g/ml; the independent fl-oz row (30.5 g / 29.5735 ml, portionRowId 81888) cross-checks at the identical 1.0313 g/ml. Catalog serving is 1 tbsp = 14.7868 ml (catalogServingMl), so derivedGramsPerServing = 1.0313 * 14.7868 = 15.25 g (exact: 244/16). Reconciliation: est 3.4 kcal vs authored 4 (delta 0.6); the food previously stayed gated only because Batch 5 scope was ml-unit foods.

Poppy-seeds serving-semantics correction slice (owner-authorized). Batch 2/4 correctly found the authored macros (1/1/1 g, 17 kcal Atwater) fail the SR tbsp portion (portionRowId 88450, 8.8 g -> est 46.2 kcal, delta 29.2 > 25): they are teaspoon-scale. The serving was corrected tbsp(1) -> tsp(1) per the tsp-semantics mini-slice pattern; the SR tsp portion (2.8 g) reconciles cleanly (est 14.7 kcal vs authored 17, delta 2.3; protein 0.50 vs 1; carbs 0.79 vs 1; fat 1.16 vs 1). Macros unchanged; only the serving semantics and the sourced gram weight changed.

Amendment A1 Batch F1 (first FNDDS match). Prior SR reason (superseded for the FNDDS pin): 'Pinned SR Legacy has no cooked polenta cup row; dry/self-rising cornmeal candidates are preparation mismatches.' FNDDS codes polenta as 'Cornmeal mush' — the record officially carries the additional description 'polenta' in food_attribute.csv (attribute row 3314100) — and the no-added-fat variant matches the authored zero-added-fat macros. Portion '1 cup, cooked' = 240 g. Reconciliation: est 139.2 kcal vs authored 141 (delta 1.8); protein 2.69 vs 3; carbs 29.93 vs 30; fat 0.67 vs 1 — all comfortably within tolerance.

Amendment A1 Batch F2. Prior SR reason (superseded for the FNDDS pin): 'Pinned SR Legacy pesto records provide 0.25 cup portions, not tbsp portions.' FNDDS 'Pesto sauce' is the as-consumed prepared/composite survey food (A1 composite eligibility) with a direct '1 tablespoon' portion = 16 g. Reconciliation: est 92.8 kcal vs authored 80 (delta 12.8); protein 1.38 vs 1; carbs 0.91 vs 1; fat 9.47 vs 8 (delta 1.47) — all within tolerance.

Amendment A1 Batch F2. Prior SR reason (superseded for the FNDDS pin): 'Pinned SR Legacy has no tzatziki tablespoon portion.' FNDDS 'Tzatziki dip' (attribute-tagged 'Greek dip') is the as-consumed prepared/composite survey food with a direct '1 tablespoon' portion = 15 g; the authored 2-tbsp serving derives 2 x 15 = 30 g. Reconciliation: est 27.3 kcal vs authored 21 (delta 6.3); protein 1.57 vs 1; carbs 1.23 vs 2; fat 1.80 vs 1 — all within tolerance.

unmatched.reason: SR Legacy has no standalone sourdough record. The only sourdough-inclusive candidate 172675 'Bread, french or vienna (includes sourdough)' carries a 139 g 'slice' portion, which fails macro reconciliation against the authored 97 kcal slice (scaled estimate 378.1 kcal, delta 281 >> 25). Left null and gated per ADR-P013 (never force-fit); revisit in a later batch with a different pinned dataset (e.g. FNDDS) if desired.

SR Legacy has a chia seed record but no tbsp/tablespoon portion row in the pinned archive; left null/gated rather than converting from ounces. FNDDS re-check (Batch F2, fndds_survey_food_csv_2024-10-31): 'Chia seeds' (2707590) exists but likewise carries no tablespoon portion (only '1 cup' = 168 g and a quantity-not-specified row); cup->tbsp volume conversion is reserved for liquids under A1, and the converted estimate would fail the kcal gate anyway (est 102.1 vs authored 128, delta 25.9 > 25). Left null/gated.

Catalog food is ground flaxseed. SR's ground 1 tbsp portion (7 g) fails macro reconciliation against the authored serving, while the passing whole-flaxseed portion is a preparation mismatch; left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F2): FNDDS 'Flax seeds' (2707588) is the whole-seed record (1 cup = 168 g, whole-seed density) with no tablespoon portion and no ground variant — the whole-vs-ground preparation mismatch persists. Left null/gated.

Pinned SR Legacy archive has no MCT-oil tablespoon portion row; left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F2): FNDDS 2021-2023 contains no MCT / medium-chain-triglyceride record at all (food.csv and food_attribute.csv both empty). Left null/gated.

Pinned SR Legacy has no nutritional-yeast tablespoon portion. Baker's yeast is a source/preparation mismatch and fails macro reconciliation; left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F2): the only FNDDS yeast record ('Yeast', 2710005) is attribute-tagged 'brewers yeast' — a different product from fortified nutritional yeast; no nutritional-yeast record exists. Rejection stands.

Pinned SR Legacy has no matching Greek-yogurt herb dressing tablespoon portion; left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F2): the only FNDDS record carrying a 'yogurt dressing' attribute is 'Fruit dressing' (2710214) — a sweet fruit-salad dressing, a semantic mismatch for a savory Greek-yogurt herb dressing; no matching record exists. Left null/gated.

SR Legacy has no basmati-specific cooked rice cup row; generic white-rice rows were rejected to avoid losing varietal specificity. Left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1, fndds_survey_food_csv_2024-10-31): no basmati-specific record either — FNDDS attribute-tags its generic 'Rice, white' records as 'basmati or jasmine rice' (deliberately not distinguished), so the generic-row rejection stands under the A1 varietal rule.

SR Legacy has no jasmine-specific cooked rice cup row; generic white-rice rows were rejected to avoid losing varietal specificity. Left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): same finding as basmati — FNDDS folds jasmine into generic 'Rice, white' via attribute tags; generic rejected under the A1 varietal rule.

Pinned SR Legacy has no cooked farro cup row; left null/gated rather than substituting another wheat grain. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS 2021-2023 contains no farro record at all (food.csv and food_attribute.csv both empty for 'farro').
Pinned SR Legacy has no cooked sorghum cup row (only non-serving syrup/flour-style candidates); left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS 2021-2023 contains no sorghum record at all.

Catalog food is whole wheat couscous; pinned SR Legacy provides generic cooked couscous only. Rejected to avoid preparation/grain mismatch; left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS likewise provides only generic 'Couscous, plain, cooked' (2708441); no whole-wheat couscous record — rejection stands.

SR Legacy does not provide a red-lentil-specific cooked cup row; generic lentils were not used for this varietal. Left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS provides only generic lentil records ('Lentils, NFS' / 'from dried') with no red-lentil-specific row or attribute; generic rejected under the A1 varietal rule.

SR Legacy does not provide a green-lentil-specific cooked cup row; generic lentils were not used for this varietal. Left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): same finding as red lentils — only generic lentil records exist; generic rejected under the A1 varietal rule.

Pinned SR Legacy has no cannellini-specific cooked cup row; white/kidney bean substitutes were rejected to avoid varietal mismatch. Left null/gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS has only generic 'White beans' records, attribute-tagged 'Cannellini' among other white beans (deliberately not distinguished); generic rejected under the A1 varietal rule.

Both SR cooked-onion records (170001 without salt, 170100 with salt) fail macro reconciliation for the authored 1-cup serving: 1 cup = 210 g gives 88-92 kcal and 20-21 g carbs vs the authored 64 kcal / 14 g (carbs delta > tolerance). Left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): 'Onions, cooked, no added fat' (2709950, 1 cup = 210 g) also fails — est 98.7 kcal / 23.0 g carbs vs authored 64 / 14. Failure under BOTH pins -> authored-data disagreement (A1 class 4); needs a per-food correction-slice decision, not a re-match.

Catalog food is "Snow peas, cooked" but the SR cooked edible-podded record (170509) fails reconciliation (64 kcal / 10.3 g carbs vs authored 40 / 7); only the RAW record passes, which contradicts the authored cooked name. Semantic mismatch left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): 'Snowpeas, fresh, cooked, no added fat' (2709983, 1 cup = 160 g) fails too — est 70.4 kcal / 12.5 g carbs vs authored 40 / 7; frozen and NS-form variants fail as well. Failure under BOTH pins -> authored-data disagreement (A1 class 4).

SR cooked leek records (168426/168535) reconcile to 32.2 kcal and 7.9 g carbs per authored 1-cup serving vs the authored 56 kcal / 13 g — outside tolerance. Left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): the sole FNDDS record 'Leeks' (2709935, 1 cup = 170 g) carries added cooking fat (3.05 g fat/100 g) and fails catastrophically — est 149.6 kcal / 5.2 g fat vs authored 56 / 0. Failure under BOTH pins -> authored-data disagreement (A1 class 4).

No SR Legacy record exists for a salad-greens mix; the single-lettuce analogs (green leaf, butterhead, iceberg, romaine) have materially different cup weights (36-57 g) and none represents a mix. Ambiguous — left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS has no mixed/spring-mix record either. Generic 'Lettuce, raw' (2709789, 1 cup = 35 g) reconciles NUMERICALLY (est 14.0 kcal vs authored 12 for the 2-cup 70 g serving) but is a single-genus lettuce NFS code, not a greens mix (spring mix includes spinach/arugula/chard) — not clearly acceptable under the A1 matching standard. Accepting the lettuce-NFS proxy would be a separate owner decision.

SR Legacy has no broccolini record; the nearest name match "Broccoli raab, raw" is a different vegetable and fails reconciliation catastrophically (8.8 kcal vs authored 53). Left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): FNDDS officially codes broccolini to 'Broccoli raab, cooked' (2709573, attribute-tagged 'broccolini'; 1 cup = 170 g), but the record fails reconciliation — est 78.2 kcal / 4.9 g carbs / 5.4 g fat vs authored 53 / 8 / 1 (the record carries added cooking fat and raab is a different vegetable). Rejection stands.

The only SR cup portion for Pomegranates, raw (169134, "0.5 cup arils" = 87 g -> 174 g/cup) fails macro reconciliation for the authored serving: estimated carbs 32.5 g vs authored 26 g (delta 6.5 > max(20%, 3 g) tolerance) even though calories pass. Left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): 'Pomegranate, raw' (2709267, 1 cup = 175 g) fails on the same axis — est carbs 32.7 g vs authored 26 (delta 6.7 > 5.2 tolerance); kcal passes. Failure under BOTH pins -> authored-data disagreement (A1 class 4).

SR Legacy 2018-04 contains no dragon fruit / pitaya / pitahaya record at all (added to FDC only in later non-SR data types). No source row exists in the pinned archive. Left null and gated. FNDDS re-check (Batch F1): 'Dragon fruit' (2709234, attribute 'pitaya', 1 cup = 180 g) EXISTS but fails reconciliation badly — est 122.4 kcal / 29.2 g carbs vs authored 56 / 13. The authored values disagree with the FNDDS record -> authored-data disagreement (A1 class 4); needs a per-food correction-slice decision, not a re-match.
